2604.16491.title: A Lightweight Transformer for Pain Recognition from Brain Activity
2604.16491.paper_summary: Pain is a multifaceted and widespread phenomenon with substantial clinical and societal burden, making reliable automated assessment a critical objective. This paper presents a lightweight transformer architecture that fuses multiple fNIRS representations through a unified tokenization mechanism, enabling joint modeling of complementary signal views without requiring modality-specific adaptations or increasing architectural complexity. The proposed token-mixing strategy preserves spatial, temporal, and time-frequency characteristics by projecting heterogeneous inputs onto a shared latent representation, using a structured segmentation scheme to control the granularity of local aggregation and global interaction. The model is evaluated on the AI4Pain dataset using stacked raw waveform and power spectral density representations of fNIRS inputs. Experimental results demonstrate competitive pain recognition performance while remaining computationally compact, making the approach suitable for real-time inference on both GPU and CPU hardware.
2604.16491.paper_summary_zh: 疼痛是一種多面向且廣泛的現象，對臨床和社會造成了重大的負擔，使得可靠的自動評估成為一個關鍵目標。這篇論文提出了一種輕量級的Transformer架構，通過統一的標記機制融合多個fNIRS表示，實現了互補信號視圖的聯合建模，而無需特定於模態的調整或增加架構的複雜性。所提出的標記混合策略通過將異質輸入投影到共享的潛在表示上，保留了空間、時間和時間頻率特徵，並使用結構化的分段方案來控制局部聚合和全局交互的粒度。該模型在AI4Pain數據集上進行評估，使用堆疊的原始波形和fNIRS輸入的功率譜密度表示。實驗結果顯示出具有競爭力的疼痛識別性能，同時保持計算的緊湊性，使得該方法適合在GPU和CPU硬件上進行實時推斷。
2604.16491.author: Stefanos Gkikas et.al.
2604.16491.authors: Stefanos Gkikas, Christian Arzate Cruz, Yu Fang, Lu Cao, Muhammad Umar Khan, Thomas Kassiotis, Giorgos Giannakakis, Raul Fernandez Rojas, Randy Gomez
